reasoning: Jerome Powell's term as Fed Chair ends in May 2026. Despite intense pressure from President Trump to resign in July 2025, Powell has emphatically and repeatedly stated he will not step down, noting that it is illegal to fire him. By late July and August 2025, the Trump administration appeared to accept this timeline, with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent confirming there is "no need" for Powell to resign immediately, and the administration focusing on a formal process to select a successor for May 2026. Furthermore, Powell just delivered what the media explicitly termed his "final" Jackson Hole speech on August 22, 2025, since his term will expire before the next symposium in August 2026. The chance of a departure before January 1, 2026, is largely constrained to a sudden severe health issue (he is 72) or an unexpected, legally unprecedented actual firing that resolves quickly, both of which are highly improbable in a four-month window (~3%).
